description: Clustered DK harmonic-address compatibility probe on the no-distance combinatorial branch, using purely relational address weights on a fixed clustered mixed-encounter seed.

run.notes: Exact-match address control with both packets on address 0.

metrics.notes: Exact-match address control with both packets on address 0.

run.notes: Exact-match packet labels with symmetry broken only in the address-field distribution.

metrics.notes: Exact-match packet labels with symmetry broken only in the address-field distribution.

run.notes: Near-match forward orientation with address difference +1 mod 4.

metrics.notes: Near-match forward orientation with address difference +1 mod 4.

run.notes: Near-match reverse orientation with address difference -1 mod 4.

metrics.notes: Near-match reverse orientation with address difference -1 mod 4.

run.notes: Second near-match control to test label-shift invariance in the near-compatible sector.

metrics.notes: Second near-match control to test label-shift invariance in the near-compatible sector.

run.notes: Clear mismatch with address separation 2 mod 4.

metrics.notes: Clear mismatch with address separation 2 mod 4.

run.notes: Label-shift control for the incompatible sector.

metrics.notes: Label-shift control for the incompatible sector.

run.notes: Randomized address-field control on the same clustered seed.

metrics.notes: Randomized address-field control on the same clustered seed.

summary.interpretive_note: Harmonic addressing behaves like a weak proto-selection rule on this clustered seed: exact-match labeling preserves the braid class, while near-match and incompatible sectors relax to smeared transfer. The visible signal is topology and sorting bias, not measurable persistence gain.
